text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Delgado, Marcus T.
Date of Birth: 1965
Sex: Male
MRN: [REDACTED]
Accession Number: SP-XXXXX
Ordering Physician: Gastroenterology / GI Endoscopy
Clinical Service: Gastroenterology
Attending Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist Name]
Resident/Fellow: [Resident Name]

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN SUBMITTED
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: "Sigmoid colon biopsy" — Multiple fragments of colonic mucosa obtained via forceps biopsy during colonoscopy.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATE OF PROCEDURE
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Colonoscopy with biopsy performed by gastroenterology. Specimen received in formalin fixative, labeled with patient name and medical record number. Specimen received fresh and placed into 10% neutral buffered formalin for fixation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY / INDICATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Male patient, age 58, presenting with change in bowel habits, rectal bleeding, and a palpable sigmoid mass identified on abdominal imaging. Colonoscopy performed for evaluation of sigmoid colon mass measuring approximately 4.5 cm. CEA markedly elevated at 487 ng/mL. Biopsy obtained from the mass for histopathological diagnosis.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "sigmoid colon biopsy," are multiple irregular fragments of pale tan-pink to hemorrhagic tissue, ranging from 0.1 to 0.4 cm in greatest dimension. The fragments are soft and friable in consistency. Aggregate tissue measures 0.8 x 0.5 x 0.2 cm. There is focal areas of surface irregularity with a granular, indurated texture consistent with sampling of a polypoid or mass lesion. No discrete normal colonic folds identifiable. All fragments entirely submitted in one cassette, labeled A1.

Total cassettes: 1 (A1).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Microscopic examination of all submitted sections reveals colonic mucosal fragments extensively replaced and infiltrated by a moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. The tumor demonstrates predominantly tubular glandular architecture with variably sized, irregularly shaped glands lined by stratified columnar epithelium exhibiting moderate nuclear pleomorphism. Nuclei are enlarged, hyperchromatic, and variably vesicular with prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are readily identified, including occasional atypical forms. There is a focal area comprising less than 10% of the sampled tumor showing a mucinous/extracellular mucin component with pools of mucin partially distending glands, but the mucinous component does not constitute ≥50% of the lesion and therefore does not qualify as a mucinous (colloid) adenocarcinoma by World Health Organization criteria.

The tumor glands are embedded within a desmoplastic stroma with associated chronic inflammatory infiltrate. Focal areas of necrotic debris ("dirty necrosis") are present within gland lumina, a characteristic feature of colorectal-type adenocarcinoma. No signet ring cells are identified. No poorly differentiated clusters or single infiltrating cells are noted to dominate the picture.

Fragments of non-neoplastic colonic mucosa at the periphery of the biopsy show no significant dysplasia or inflammatory bowel disease pattern. No granulomas identified. No viral cytopathic changes.

Grade: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (Grade 2, ISUP/WHO 2019).

Note: Lymphovascular invasion and perineural invasion cannot be definitively assessed on this superficial biopsy material; clinical staging and resection specimen evaluation will be required for complete staging.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGMOID COLON, COLONOSCOPIC BIOPSY:

  - ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 2)
  - Predominantly tubular architecture with focal mucinous features (mucinous component <10%; does NOT meet criteria for mucinous adenocarcinoma)
  - No signet ring cells identified
  - See ancillary studies below

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC) — Mismatch Repair (MMR) Protein Panel:
Performed on block A1 using standardized automated immunostaining platform (Ventana BenchMark ULTRA). Positive and negative controls performed concurrently and are satisfactory.

  MLH1 (clone ES05):    RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells
  MSH2 (clone FE11):    RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells
  MSH6 (clone EP49):    RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells
  PMS2 (clone EP51):    RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells

Interpretation: No loss of mismatch repair protein expression identified. This immunophenotype is consistent with a MISMATCH REPAIR-PROFICIENT (MMR-p) tumor, and is consistent with MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS) status. Note: PCR-based MSI testing is recommended for definitive classification and has been ordered concurrently (see comment below).

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY — Tumor Lineage Markers:

  CDX2 (clone AMC462):   POSITIVE — diffuse strong nuclear staining in tumor cells
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):   POSITIVE — diffuse cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30): NEGATIVE — no appreciable staining in tumor cells
  SATB2 (clone EP228):   POSITIVE — diffuse moderate-to-strong nuclear staining in tumor cells
  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):   Approximately 65% of tumor nuclei positive (high proliferative index)

Interpretation: The immunoprofile (CDX2+, CK20+, CK7-, SATB2+) is characteristic of a colorectal primary adenocarcinoma. The pattern is not supportive of a pancreatobiliary, upper gastrointestinal, or pulmonary primary.

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) BY PCR — STATUS: PENDING
PCR-based microsatellite instability analysis using the NCI-recommended 5-marker panel (BAT-25, BAT-26, D5S346, D2S123, D17S250) has been ordered on block A1 and is currently pending. Results will be reported as an addendum upon completion. (See note: IHC MMR profile above is consistent with MSS; PCR correlation in progress.)

HER2 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY — STATUS: PENDING
HER2 IHC (clone 4B5) has been ordered and is pending. Results to be reported as an addendum.

MOLECULAR / MUTATIONAL ANALYSIS:
Tissue has been triaged and FFPE block reserved for potential extended molecular/NGS testing as clinically indicated. Specific molecular testing (e.g., RAS/RAF/NRAS/KRAS mutational analysis, BRAF V600E) to be ordered by clinical team and will be reported separately by the molecular pathology laboratory.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. This moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with retained MMR protein expression and a colorectal immunoprofile (CDX2+/CK20+/CK7-/SATB2+) is consistent with a primary colorectal adenocarcinoma arising in the sigmoid colon, in the appropriate clinical context.

2. The mucinous component observed on this biopsy is focal (<10%) and does not meet the threshold for classification as a mucinous adenocarcinoma (which requires ≥50% extracellular mucin by WHO criteria). The clinical significance of minor mucinous features in otherwise tubular colorectal adenocarcinoma remains uncertain.

3. Ki-67 labeling index of approximately 65% indicates a high proliferative rate, consistent with moderately to poorly differentiated colorectal carcinoma biology.

4. Definitive pathologic staging will require assessment of the resection specimen (when/if performed), including evaluation of tumor depth of invasion (pT), lymph node status (pN), and assessment of lymphovascular and perineural invasion.

5. MSI PCR results and HER2 IHC results are pending; addendum report to follow.

6. Correlation with clinical, endoscopic, and radiological findings is recommended.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST ATTESTATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
I have reviewed the gross description, microscopic sections, and ancillary study results (where available) for this case. The above diagnosis reflects my interpretation of the histologic material.

Electronic signature — Attending Pathologist
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

------------------------------------------------------------------------
*** ADDENDUM (PENDING — to be issued upon receipt of PCR-MSI and HER2 IHC results) ***
------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ADDENDUM ISSUED SUBSEQUENTLY]

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) BY PCR — FINAL:
Using the NCI 5-marker panel (BAT-25, BAT-26, D5S346, D2S123, D17S250), all five microsatellite loci demonstrate no instability. Zero of five markers show instability.
Conclusion: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
This result is concordant with the intact MMR protein expression by IHC (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2 all retained).

HER2 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY — FINAL:
HER2 IHC (clone 4B5, Ventana): Score 1+ (incomplete, faint basal/lateral membrane staining in <10% of tumor cells).
Interpretation: HER2 1+ — NEGATIVE for HER2 overexpression by IHC scoring criteria (ASCO/CAP 2021 guidelines for colorectal carcinoma; score 0 or 1+ = negative). HER2 FISH/ISH not indicated based on IHC 1+ result.

Addendum electronically signed — Attending Pathologist
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
